data.title: Perrigo recalls baby formula sold at CVS, H-E-B stores
data.description:
Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) has voluntarily recalled 16,500 cans of its store brand Premium Infant Formula with Iron Milk-Based Powder that were shipped to H-E-B Grocery Company and CVS (CVS).

The healthcare products company said in a filing on Thursday that the recall was conducted on the retailer and warehouse level. The product was shipped to CVS between Feb. 6 and Feb. 11 and to H-E-B between Feb. 2 and May 13.

The recalled formula had been sent to H-E-B in Texas and to CVS in Texas, Florida, California, South Carolina, Virginia, Indiana, Tennessee, New Jersey, Michigan, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Missouri. Perrigo has asked H-E-B and CVS to examine their inventories and isolate the product.

According to a company statement posted on the FDA’s website, the product was voluntarily recalled after higher than permitted levels of Vitamin D were detected through routine testing. No reports of adverse events to date have been attributed to the elevated levels of Vitamin D.

Perrigo said in its filing that the recall is not expected to have a material impact on the company or the 2024 financial outlook it issued on Aug. 2.

data.title: Evercore ISI expresses concerns around an un-inverted 2s/10s yield curve
data.description:
Evercore ISI spotlighted that the last two times the yield curve between the U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) and the U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) went from being inverted to un-inverted, a noticeable market selloff followed within a 12-24 month period.

Earlier this week, the 2s/10s curve, which has long been inverted, briefly flashed to being un-inverted.

“The 2/10s have broken out and are just 8 bps from freedom after 2 years of subterranean subterfuge. While there is ‘no bell which rings at the top’, the last two times this occurred the S&P fell -43% and -53% over the subsequent 12-24 months. This is not a timing tool, but it’s still not bullish,” Evercore ISI stated in an investor note on Friday.

Back on Monday, the 2s10s yield curve briefly un-inverted back to normalization for the first time in over two years, but Wall Street is still waiting for the first day for the curve to actually close at an un-inverted level.

As of Friday, the shorter end US2Y is lower by 2 basis points to 4.02% while the longer end 10Y has dipped 6 basis points to 3.93%. Moreover, the yield curve between the two instruments currently sits at -8 basis points.

See the longer-term chart below of the 2s/10s yield curve, which highlights the initial start of the inversion back on July 5, 2022.

For market participants closely monitoring the moves in Treasuries and bonds, they may look to further analyze Treasury focused exchange-traded funds and other fixed income ETFs as a proxy investment. See some popular listed funds below:

Treasury ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (NYSEARCA:AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).

Furthermore, see how other yields trade across the entire yield curve here.

data.title: Savers Value Village dives to record low on Q2 miss, Goldman downgrade
data.description:
Savers Value Village (NYSE:SVV) shares were spiraling lower on Friday as a combination of a miss on Q2 earnings and a subsequent downgrade by Goldman Sachs drove the stock to a record low.

Goldman downgraded the stock to Neutral from Buy and cut its target price by 29% to $10 as the investment bank has lost conviction in Savers Value Village’s (SVV) ability to protect profitability amid strengthening headwinds in the Canadian market.

Goldman’s Buy rating was predicated on the company’s market share leadership position in a structurally growing category, as well as Savers’ flexible operating model which would insulate profits. But while Goldman continues to view the company as an attractive market-share gainer, it moves to the sidelines with a Neutral rating as the outlook for sales and margin outperformance has diminished.

During the second quarter, Savers Value Village (SVV) experienced softening sales in its Canadian business, “reflecting the ongoing challenging economic environment in Canada.” As a result of these trends, the company updated its FY24 guidance to reflect lower-than-expected transactions from its Canadian segment.

The company now expects sales to be between $1.53B to $1.56B from initial guidance of $1.57B to $1.59B and below the consensus estimate of $1.57B.

Comparable store sales have been revised to down 1% to up 1% from 2% to 3% growth. Adjusted net income is now projected at $82B to $96B from $126B to $133B, previously, and adjusted EBITDA of $290M to $310M from $330M to $340M.

In the current quarter, Savers Value Village earned an adjusted profit of $0.14 per share, down from $0.22 in the same quarter last year and 6 cents below expectations. Net sales were up 2% to $386.7M, but below the consensus estimate of $389.7M.

Shares were down as much as 19% on Friday.

data.title: Array Tech tumbles to two-year low after big guidance cut; Roth MKM downgrades
data.description:
Array Technologies (NASDAQ:ARRY) -21.8% to a nearly two-and-a-half year low in Friday's trading despite beating Q2 adjusted earnings estimates, as the solar tracking equipment company cut its full-year guidance for earnings and revenues.

Q2 net income fell to $25.7M, or $0.08/share, from $65.2M, or $0.34/share, in the year-earlier quarter, with revenues down by nearly half to $255M but ahead of analyst expectations.

But Array (ARRY) said it expects a drop in volumes throughout the year due to changes in expected customer project timing, causing it to cut its FY 2024 forecast for adjusted earnings to $0.64-$0.74/share from prior guidance of $1.00-$1.15/share, and revenues to $900M-$1B from $1.25B-$1.4B.

Roth MKM analyst Philip Shen downgraded Array (ARRY) to Neutral from Buy with an $8 price target, slashed from $20, seeing a risk of more project pushouts, especially from 2025 into 2026.

Array's (ARRY) guidance cut was "widely expected by investors and expectations were low, nonetheless a 28% reduction probably wasn't on anyone's bingo card," Piper Sandler's Kashy Harrison writes, adding visibility is limited in the current environment and "it is difficult for anyone to have conviction on the forward trajectory."

data.title: Trump appears open to decriminalizing marijuana
data.description:
• Former President Donald Trump said Thursday that he is starting to believe that marijuana should be decriminalized given legalization in many states.
  • At a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago resort in Palm Beach, Fla., the Republican presidential nominee said, according to Marijuana Moment. "As we legalize it throughout the country — whether that's a good thing or a bad thing— it's awfully hard to have people all over the jails that are in jail right now for something that’s legal."
  • As a Florida resident, Trump will have the opportunity to vote on a proposed constitutional amendment that would legalize recreational marijuana in the state. He said he would soon address this issue. Gov. Ron DeSantis (Rep.) is again legalization.
  • As president, in 2018, Trump rescinded the Cole Memorandum, put into effect by former President Obama in 2013 that directed federal prosecutors not to pursue many marijuana offenses.
  • Multi-state operators: Ascend Wellness (OTCQX:AAWH), Curaleaf Holdings (OTCPK:CURLF), Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF), Green Thumb Industries (OTCQX:GTBIF), Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF), Ayr Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF), Verano Holdings (OTCQX:VRNOF), and Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF).
  • Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (YOLO), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (NYSEARCA:MJ), and Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (CNBS).

data.title: United Internet AG reports 1H results
data.description:
• United Internet AG press release (OTCPK:UDIRF): 1H Sales of €3,099.9M (+2.8% Y/Y).

  • On August 2, 2024, United Internet adjusted its full-year guidance and now expects sales of approx. €6.4B in 2024 (previous guidance: approx. €6.5B; 2023: €6.213B). EBITDA is expected to amount to approx. €1.38B, including out-of-period expenses of -€14.3M, or approx. €1.39B without out-of-period expenses (previous guidance: approx. €1.42B; 2023: €1.30B).

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow trade to the downside along with yields as they inch lower
data.description:
Major market averages are trade lower on Friday, a day after investors found some comfort from a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down 6 basis points to at 3.92%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.01%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

"Barely a week since recessionary concerns rocked markets, investors have regained some poise, with share prices largely recouping the steep losses of the last few days," Richard Hunter, Interactive Investor said.

"Markets are closing out the week in a Disney paradise after having starting the week completely frozen! The buoyant market mood over the past 24 hours was given a significant boost by a decent U.S. jobless claims report," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The better outturn pointed to enough strength in the U.S. labor market not to suggest that it is not falling apart. It certainly provided some evidence to the view that part of the weak payrolls may have been weather-related effects, Reid added.

Traders were focused on the initial jobless claims report, as they looked to gauge the labor market after last week's disappointing jobs report.

The economic calendar is light today, with only the Baker-Hughes rig count on tap.
